title: Childcare Workers and Teaching Assistants
exposure_rationale: Childcare and teaching assistants work is fundamentally interpersonal and physical — caring for children, maintaining safe environments, direct interaction. AI has minimal impact on hands-on caregiving.

title: Accountants
exposure_rationale: Accounting is fundamentally digital — data analysis, regulatory compliance, auditing, and reporting. AI is already automating routine bookkeeping, tax computation, and anomaly detection, significantly restructuring the profession.

title: Administrative Staff
exposure_rationale: Administrative work is almost entirely digital — data entry, document processing, scheduling, correspondence. AI can already handle most of these routine information-processing tasks.

title: Marketing, PR and Sales Advisors
exposure_rationale: Marketing, PR, and sales advisory is heavily digital — content creation, data analysis, campaign management, copywriting. AI is already transforming all of these domains.

title: Chief Executives and General Managers
exposure_rationale: General directors combine strategic decision-making, stakeholder management, and organizational leadership. AI assists with data analysis and reporting, but leadership, vision, and human judgment remain essential.

title: Pharmacy Assistants
exposure_rationale: Pharmacy assistants combine prescription processing (increasingly automated) with patient counseling and physical dispensing. AI-driven drug interaction checking and automated dispensing systems increase exposure.

title: Architects
exposure_rationale: Architecture combines creative design (increasingly AI-assisted for rendering, generative design, and BIM optimization) with client relationships and site knowledge. The digital design component faces high exposure.

title: Physicians and Doctors
exposure_rationale: Physicians combine diagnostic reasoning (increasingly AI-assisted via imaging and clinical decision support) with physical examination, procedures, and patient relationships that require human presence.

title: Assembly Workers
exposure_rationale: Assembly work is primarily physical and hands-on, involving repetitive manual tasks on production lines. While some automation exists, the varied and physical nature of assembly in many Dutch manufacturing settings limits AI impact.

title: Authors and Linguists
exposure_rationale: Writing and linguistics is almost entirely digital — content creation, translation, editing, language analysis. AI writing tools and machine translation are rapidly advancing in these exact domains.

title: Auto Mechanics
exposure_rationale: Auto mechanics work is fundamentally hands-on, diagnosing and repairing physical vehicles. While diagnostic software is increasingly AI-assisted, the core repair work requires physical skill and presence.

title: Bakers
exposure_rationale: Baking is almost entirely physical and manual, requiring hands-on preparation, monitoring, and craftsmanship. AI has minimal direct impact on the core artisanal and production work.

title: Mobile Machine Operators
exposure_rationale: Operating mobile machinery (cranes, excavators, forklifts) is physical, requires spatial awareness and real-time decision-making in unpredictable environments. Autonomous machinery is emerging but adoption is slow.

title: Business Analysts and Management Consultants
exposure_rationale: Management consulting is knowledge work involving analysis, strategy, and communication — all digital domains. However, client relationships and organizational politics require human judgment.

title: Visual Artists
exposure_rationale: Visual artists face exposure from AI image generation, but physical mediums (sculpture, painting) and original artistic vision provide protection. Digital art and illustration face much higher exposure.

title: Policy Advisors
exposure_rationale: Policy advisory work is predominantly digital — research, analysis, writing reports, developing recommendations. AI is increasingly capable in these domains, though political judgment remains human.

title: Security Personnel
exposure_rationale: Security personnel require physical presence for patrol, access control, and incident response. AI surveillance aids monitoring but cannot replace on-site human security.

title: Librarians and Curators
exposure_rationale: Librarians and curators combine information management (digital, AI-exposed) with public service, exhibition curation, and community programming that require human judgment and presence.

title: Biologists and Natural Scientists
exposure_rationale: Natural science involves both lab/field work (physical) and data analysis/modeling (digital). AI is transforming computational biology and data analysis, but fieldwork and experimental design require human judgment.

title: Bookkeepers
exposure_rationale: Bookkeeping is routine digital information processing — recording transactions, reconciling accounts, preparing financial statements. AI and automation are rapidly taking over these tasks.

title: Accounting Clerks
exposure_rationale: Bookkeeping assistants handle routine financial data entry and processing — one of the most automatable categories of work. AI tools already handle much of this.

title: Finishing Construction Workers
exposure_rationale: Finishing construction work (plastering, tiling, flooring) is highly physical and requires manual dexterity in varied environments. AI has essentially no impact on these hands-on tasks.

title: Structural Construction Workers
exposure_rationale: Structural construction work is almost entirely physical labor in unpredictable outdoor environments. AI and automation have minimal impact on core tasks like bricklaying and concrete work.

title: Bus Drivers and Tram Operators
exposure_rationale: Bus and tram driving faces moderate AI exposure from autonomous vehicle technology, but the timeline for full automation of public transit in urban environments is long. Passenger safety duties add protection.

title: Outbound Call Center Agents and Other Sales Staff
exposure_rationale: Outbound calling and sales is routine communication work increasingly handled by AI. Automated dialers, AI chatbots, and personalized outreach tools are rapidly replacing human agents.

title: Car, Taxi and Van Drivers
exposure_rationale: Taxi and delivery driving faces growing exposure from autonomous vehicle technology. While full self-driving is not yet deployed at scale, the trajectory clearly points toward automation.

title: Database and Network Specialists
exposure_rationale: Database and network administration is fully digital work involving system configuration, monitoring, and optimization — all areas where AI automation is rapidly advancing.

title: Deck Officers and Pilots
exposure_rationale: Aviation and maritime officers face moderate exposure — autopilot and navigation AI assist significantly, but human judgment for safety decisions and unusual situations remains critical.

title: Executive Secretaries
exposure_rationale: Executive assistants primarily handle digital tasks — scheduling, correspondence, document preparation, travel booking. AI assistants are increasingly capable of these coordination tasks.

title: Secondary School Teachers (General Subjects)
exposure_rationale: Secondary school teaching combines knowledge delivery (increasingly AI-assisted) with classroom management, mentoring, and interpersonal guidance that requires human presence and judgment.

title: Secondary School Teachers (Vocational Subjects)
exposure_rationale: Vocational education combines hands-on practical instruction with theoretical teaching. The physical demonstration and workshop supervision component limits AI exposure.

title: Higher Education Lecturers and Professors
exposure_rationale: Higher education involves research (data analysis, writing — high AI exposure), teaching (moderate exposure), and student mentoring (low exposure). The research component drives overall exposure up.

title: Electricians and Electronics Technicians
exposure_rationale: Electrical installation and repair is hands-on work requiring physical presence and manual skill. AI may assist with diagnostics and planning, but core installation work remains physical.

title: Electrical Engineers
exposure_rationale: Electrical engineering is predominantly knowledge work — circuit design, simulation, programming — all digital domains where AI is rapidly advancing. Some physical testing and site work provides a floor.

title: Financial Specialists and Economists
exposure_rationale: Financial analysis and economics is predominantly digital — data modeling, forecasting, reporting, research. AI excels at pattern recognition and quantitative analysis in financial data.

title: Photographers and Interior Designers
exposure_rationale: Photography and interior design are increasingly digital — editing, 3D visualization, style generation. AI tools for image editing and design generation are transforming both fields.

title: Physiotherapists
exposure_rationale: Physiotherapy is fundamentally hands-on — manual therapy, exercise guidance, physical assessment. The interpersonal and physical nature of treatment limits AI exposure significantly.

title: IT User Support
exposure_rationale: IT support is predominantly digital — troubleshooting, ticketing, remote assistance. AI chatbots and automated diagnostics are already handling increasing volumes of support queries.

title: Specialist Nurses
exposure_rationale: Specialized nursing combines clinical assessment and care planning (some AI exposure) with direct patient care, physical procedures, and emotional support that require human presence.

title: Graphic Designers and Product Designers
exposure_rationale: Graphic and product design is predominantly digital work — creating visual content, layouts, branding. AI design tools (Midjourney, DALL-E, Figma AI) are already transforming the field.

title: Gardeners, Horticulturists and Growers
exposure_rationale: Horticulture and gardening is hands-on outdoor work requiring plant knowledge and physical labor. While greenhouse automation exists, much of the work is in varied outdoor environments.

title: Construction and Industrial Helpers
exposure_rationale: Construction and industrial helpers perform basic physical labor — carrying materials, site cleanup, assisting skilled workers. These tasks are entirely physical with minimal AI exposure.

title: Agricultural Helpers
exposure_rationale: Agricultural helpers perform basic physical farm labor — harvesting, planting, animal care. This is among the most physical occupations with minimal AI exposure.

title: Engineers (non-electrical)
exposure_rationale: Engineering is increasingly digital: CAD, simulation, optimization, and project management are all AI-exposed. Physical site inspections and hands-on problem-solving provide some protection.

title: Journalists
exposure_rationale: Journalism is digital knowledge work — research, writing, editing, fact-checking. AI can already generate news articles, summarize information, and assist investigative research.

title: Legal Professionals
exposure_rationale: Legal work is predominantly digital — contract review, legal research, document drafting, compliance analysis. AI legal tools are already transforming routine legal work.

title: Hairdressers and Beauty Specialists
exposure_rationale: Hairdressing and beauty work is entirely hands-on and interpersonal — cutting hair, applying treatments, personal consultation. AI has essentially no impact on these physical services.

title: Cashiers
exposure_rationale: Cashier work is being automated through self-checkout, contactless payment, and automated retail. While some customer interaction remains, the core transactional task is highly automatable.

title: Waiters and Bar Staff
exposure_rationale: Waiting and bar staff work is physical and interpersonal — serving food and drinks, taking orders, engaging with customers. While ordering kiosks exist, the hospitality element remains human.

title: Kitchen Helpers
exposure_rationale: Kitchen helpers perform basic physical tasks — washing dishes, preparing ingredients, cleaning. This is manual work with minimal AI exposure.

title: Chefs and Cooks
exposure_rationale: Cooking is physical, creative, and requires real-time sensory judgment. While recipe databases and kitchen management software exist, the core craft of cooking is human.

title: Laboratory Technicians
exposure_rationale: Lab technicians combine physical sample handling and equipment operation with digital data analysis and reporting. AI is advancing in diagnostic automation and image analysis.

title: Loaders, Unloaders and Shelf Stackers
exposure_rationale: Loading, unloading, and shelf stocking is physical labor. Warehouse automation is advancing, but many environments are too varied for full robotic replacement.

title: Farmers and Foresters
exposure_rationale: Farming and forestry involves physical outdoor work, but precision agriculture with GPS, drones, and AI-optimized planting/harvesting is increasing. The blend creates moderate-low exposure.

title: Welders and Sheet Metal Workers
exposure_rationale: Welding and sheet metal work is skilled physical labor. While robotic welding exists in factories, much Dutch welding work is in construction and maintenance requiring human presence and adaptability.

title: Primary School Teachers
exposure_rationale: Primary school teaching is heavily interpersonal — young children need human guidance, emotional support, and physical supervision. AI may assist with lesson planning and assessment, but core teaching remains human.

title: Plumbers and Pipefitters
exposure_rationale: Plumbing and pipefitting requires physical presence in buildings, manual dexterity, and problem-solving in varied environments. AI has limited impact on this hands-on trade.

title: Social Workers
exposure_rationale: Social workers provide interpersonal counseling, crisis intervention, and community support. While case management and reporting are digital, the core therapeutic relationship is human.

title: Machine Mechanics
exposure_rationale: Machine installation and repair requires hands-on mechanical work and troubleshooting in physical environments. AI-assisted diagnostics help, but core work remains physical.

title: ICT Managers
exposure_rationale: IT managers work is predominantly digital — overseeing technology strategy, vendor management, project planning. AI is transforming IT operations, making management more data-driven and potentially requiring fewer managers.

title: Retail and Wholesale Managers
exposure_rationale: Retail and wholesale managers need physical store/warehouse presence for operations. The analytical and administrative side faces AI exposure, but on-site management remains human.

title: Specialized Services Managers
exposure_rationale: Specialized service managers handle strategic planning, client relationships, and team leadership. AI assists with data analysis and operational optimization, but human judgment drives strategy.

title: Hospitality Managers
exposure_rationale: Hospitality managers require constant physical presence — overseeing food service, guest relations, staff management in real-time. While AI aids with booking and inventory, the core role is hands-on.

title: Logistics Managers
exposure_rationale: Logistics managers work is increasingly data-driven — supply chain optimization, route planning, inventory management. AI and algorithmic tools are central to modern logistics management.

title: Education Managers
exposure_rationale: Education managers combine administrative work (budgets, scheduling, reporting — AI-exposed) with interpersonal leadership, stakeholder management, and institutional governance.

title: Production Managers
exposure_rationale: Production managers need physical factory/site presence for operations oversight, quality control, and safety. The planning and reporting component faces AI exposure.

title: Sales and Marketing Managers
exposure_rationale: Sales and marketing management is heavily digital — campaign analytics, CRM, market research, content strategy. AI is transforming marketing optimization and customer targeting.

title: Managers n.e.c.
exposure_rationale: Unspecified managers perform a mix of strategic planning, team leadership, and operational oversight. The knowledge work component faces moderate-to-high AI exposure.

title: Business and Administrative Services Managers
exposure_rationale: Business and administrative service managers handle primarily digital work — process optimization, financial oversight, reporting. AI is increasingly automating these management functions.

title: Healthcare Institution Managers
exposure_rationale: Healthcare facility managers combine administrative work (budgets, compliance, staffing — AI-exposed) with hands-on institutional management requiring physical presence and human judgment.

title: Printing and Craft Workers
exposure_rationale: Printing and craft work is increasingly digitized. Pre-press and design work face high AI exposure, while physical operation of presses is moderate. The digital component is growing.

title: Medical Practice Assistants
exposure_rationale: Medical practice assistants handle both patient interaction (physical) and administrative tasks (scheduling, records — AI-exposed). The mix of duties creates moderate overall exposure.

title: Medical Specialists
exposure_rationale: Medical specialists (dental hygienists, opticians, etc.) combine specialized clinical knowledge with hands-on patient treatment. AI assists diagnostics but physical treatment remains human.

title: Metal Workers and Construction Workers
exposure_rationale: Metalworking and construction fabrication is physical skilled labor involving cutting, shaping, and assembling metal components. Automation exists in factories but much work is custom and on-site.

title: Furniture Makers, Tailors and Upholsterers
exposure_rationale: Furniture making, tailoring, and upholstery are craft-based, requiring hands-on skill and artistic judgment with physical materials. AI has minimal impact on these artisanal occupations.

title: Military Occupations
exposure_rationale: Military roles require physical fitness, operational readiness, and real-time decision-making in unpredictable environments. While AI aids intelligence and logistics, core military duties remain human.

title: Educational Specialists and Other Teachers
exposure_rationale: Educational specialists combine curriculum development and instructional design (digital, AI-exposed) with teaching practice (human-centric). The design and research component is increasingly automatable.

title: Civil Servants
exposure_rationale: Government officials handle primarily administrative and policy work — document processing, regulation, correspondence. These digital tasks are increasingly automatable.

title: Government Administrators
exposure_rationale: Government administrators combine policy-making and strategic decisions (requiring political judgment) with administrative oversight. AI assists analysis but governance remains human.

title: Police and Fire Service
exposure_rationale: Police and firefighters require physical presence, rapid response, and real-time decision-making in dangerous, unpredictable environments. AI aids dispatch and analysis but cannot replace frontline response.

title: Police Inspectors
exposure_rationale: Police inspectors combine investigative work (increasingly digital — data analysis, surveillance) with field operations and interpersonal skills for interrogation and leadership.

title: Process Operators
exposure_rationale: Process operators monitor and control industrial processes. While AI is increasingly used in process optimization and predictive maintenance, operators still need physical presence for safety and intervention.

title: Quality Controllers
exposure_rationale: Quality control involves both physical inspection and data analysis. AI-powered visual inspection and quality monitoring systems are rapidly advancing, increasing exposure for this traditionally hands-on role.

title: Production Supervisors (Industry and Construction)
exposure_rationale: Production managers combine knowledge work (planning, scheduling, reporting) with physical presence on factory floors and construction sites. The management/admin side faces AI exposure while the physical oversight does not.

title: Production Machine Operators
exposure_rationale: Operating production machinery is physical work requiring presence and monitoring. Some automation is replacing simpler machine operation, but varied production environments limit full AI takeover.

title: Psychologists and Sociologists
exposure_rationale: Psychology and sociology combine research and analysis (digital, AI-exposed) with therapeutic practice and human interaction. The research component drives exposure up while therapy limits it.

title: Radio and TV Technicians
exposure_rationale: Broadcast technicians combine hands-on equipment operation with digital signal processing. While AI is transforming content production, the physical equipment operation provides some protection.

title: Receptionists and Telephone Operators
exposure_rationale: Reception and telephone work involves routine communication tasks — answering calls, directing inquiries, scheduling. AI chatbots and virtual receptionists are already widely deployed.

title: Travel Guides and Tour Operators
exposure_rationale: Travel guides combine knowledge delivery (AI can provide information) with interpersonal engagement and physical accompaniment. AI travel apps handle information while guides provide experience.

title: Painters and Metal Sprayers
exposure_rationale: Painting and metal spraying is physical work requiring manual skill and physical presence on varied job sites. AI has essentially no impact on these manual trades.

title: Cleaners
exposure_rationale: Cleaning work is physical labor in varied environments. While robotic vacuum cleaners exist, most professional cleaning requires human adaptability and thoroughness.

title: Secretaries
exposure_rationale: Secretarial work involves typing, filing, scheduling, and correspondence — routine digital tasks that AI is already capable of handling at high quality.

title: Butchers
exposure_rationale: Butchery is physical, hands-on work involving cutting and preparing meat. While industrial meat processing uses some automation, the skilled craft aspect remains highly manual.

title: Social Workers, Group and Residential Care Workers
exposure_rationale: Group workers and residential counselors provide direct interpersonal care, guidance, and supervision. The work is fundamentally about human connection and physical presence in care settings.

title: Software and Application Developers
exposure_rationale: Software development is almost entirely digital — writing code, debugging, designing systems, reviewing pull requests. AI coding assistants are already transforming productivity, and the trajectory is steep.

title: HR and Career Development Specialists
exposure_rationale: HR specialists work is largely digital — screening resumes, writing job descriptions, analyzing workforce data, managing HR systems. AI recruitment tools are already transforming the field.

title: Sports Instructors
exposure_rationale: Sports instruction is fundamentally physical and interpersonal — demonstrating techniques, providing real-time feedback, motivating participants. AI has limited impact on in-person physical coaching.

title: Civil and Natural Science Technicians
exposure_rationale: Technical specialists combine fieldwork and lab work with digital analysis and reporting. The analytical component faces AI exposure while hands-on technical work remains human-dependent.

title: Carpenters
exposure_rationale: Carpentry requires physical skill, spatial reasoning, and hands-on work with wood in varied environments. While CNC and design software aid planning, core carpentry work remains manual.

title: Transport Planners and Logistics Staff
exposure_rationale: Logistics planning is data-intensive digital work — route optimization, inventory management, scheduling. AI and algorithmic optimization are already central to modern logistics.

title: Performing Artists
exposure_rationale: Performing artists (musicians, actors, dancers) work is fundamentally physical and live. While AI can generate music and digital performances, live performance requires human presence and artistry.

title: Livestock Farmers
exposure_rationale: Livestock farming combines physical animal care with data-driven herd management. AI aids health monitoring and feeding optimization, but daily animal husbandry remains hands-on.

title: Retail Sales Assistants
exposure_rationale: Retail sales requires physical presence, customer interaction, and product knowledge in a physical store environment. While AI aids inventory and recommendations, face-to-face service remains central.

title: Personal Service Workers
exposure_rationale: Other personal service providers offer direct interpersonal services requiring physical presence and human connection. AI has limited impact on face-to-face personal services.

title: Nurses (MBO)
exposure_rationale: Practical nursing is hands-on patient care — administering medication, wound care, patient monitoring, emotional support. While documentation is digital, the core caregiving work is physical and interpersonal.

title: Sales Representatives and Buyers
exposure_rationale: Sales representatives and buyers combine relationship management with data analysis and negotiation. AI assists with pricing, demand forecasting, and CRM, but relationship work remains human.

title: Care Workers
exposure_rationale: Care workers provide direct personal care — bathing, feeding, mobility assistance. This is among the most physical and interpersonal healthcare roles with minimal AI exposure.

title: Truck Drivers
exposure_rationale: Truck driving faces significant AI exposure from autonomous driving technology. While full autonomy for long-haul is advancing, last-mile delivery and complex urban routes remain challenging.

title: Refuse Collectors and Newspaper Deliverers
exposure_rationale: Waste collection and newspaper delivery are physical outdoor tasks. Automated garbage trucks are being tested, but varied residential environments limit full automation.

title: Retailers and Retail Team Leaders
exposure_rationale: Retail managers need physical store presence for supervision, merchandising, and customer handling. The administrative side faces AI exposure while the in-store management does not.

title: Business Service Providers
exposure_rationale: Business services involve analysis, consulting, and client communication — predominantly digital knowledge work where AI tools are increasingly effective.
